# Critique of Practical Reason

*[Kant](../philosophers/kant.md) · 1788*

The second of the three Critiques, it establishes that reason can determine the will through the moral law alone, independently of any interest. It sets out the postulates of practical reason: freedom, the immortality of the soul, and God.
